title: error handling a packed integer array
body:
This was tested with the latest Verilator from Git.

I tried to write a packed array of `int` (actually as a module port):
```
int [1:0] a;
```
And I got an error.
```
$ verilator --lint-only tests/InterfaceArrayAsPort/top.sv
%Error: /home/ijeras/VLSI/yosys-uhdm-plugin-integration/UHDM-integration-tests/tests/InterfaceArrayAsPort/top.sv:5:48: syntax error, unexpected '[', expecting IDENTIFIER or do or final or randomize
    5 | module dut(sim_sram_if ss_if [1:0], output int [1:0] a);
      |                                                ^
%Error: /home/ijeras/VLSI/yosys-uhdm-plugin-integration/UHDM-integration-tests/tests/InterfaceArrayAsPort/top.sv:10:23: syntax error, unexpected '[', expecting IDENTIFIER or do or final or randomize
   10 | module top(output int [1:0] o);
      |                       ^
%Error: Exiting due to 2 error(s)
```
It is possible I messed up the syntax, but I tried similar things with custom types so the syntax is probably correct.
